exercises.name: Dumbbell Elevated Lateral Lunge
exercises.description:
Stand beside a low elevated surface, such as a step or platform, holding a dumbbell in the hand on the same side as the leg that you will use to extend. 

Place that foot on the platform while keeping the other foot flat on the floor. Keep your chest up and core engaged. Push your hips back and bend the knee of the leg on the floor, lowering your body into a lateral lunge while the elevated leg extends straight to the side. 

Push through the heel of the leg on the floor to return to the starting position. Keep the opposite hand on your hip and repeat for reps before switching sides.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Exercises - DB Lateral Lunge
exercises.description: Dumbbell Exercises - DB Lateral Lunge

exercises.name: Dumbbell External Rotation on Side
exercises.description:
1.Lie on your side propped, up on one hand holding a dumbbell, palm facing the body. The other hand on the floor with arm straight.

2.Raise the dumbbell up and towards the ceiling, rotating at the shoulder and keeping your elbow at 90 degrees with your upper arm against your side.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Flat Bench Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1.Lie on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended with a slight bend in your elbows.

2.Lower the dumbbells to the sides of your body in an arc-like motion until your elbows are at the same level of the bench. 

3.Bring the weights back above your chest.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Floor Press
exercises.description:
1.Lie on the floor holding dumbbells at shoulder level with your elbows bent and your palms facing forward.

2.Press the dumbbells straight up over your chest, arms straight, keeping your palms facing forward.

3.Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder level, returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Forward Lunge
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright with dumbbells in each hand down your sides.

2.Lunge forward with your left leg, landing on your heel first.  Lower your body by bending at your knee and left hip,  while lowering the right knee until it almost touches the floor.

3.Return to the starting position by pushing on the left leg.

4.Repeat with right leg and alternate between your left and right legs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Forward Lunge and Curl
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding dumbbells by your sides with arms straight.

2.Step forward, dropping your back knee toward the floor with your weight on your front leg and raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, bending your elbows.

3.Push off your front foot, lower the dumbbells and return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side

exercises.name: Dumbbell Forward Lunge into Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, step forward into a lunge, then curl the weights to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Forward Lunge Single Arm Press
exercises.alternateName: DB forward lunge SA shoulder press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright with one hand holding a dumbbell at shoulder level with your elbow bent, wrist facing the body. The other hand for balance.

2.Step forward, dropping your back knee and leaning your torso slightly forward. At the same time, press the dumbbell overhead, rotating your wrist away from the body, extending your arm fully.

3.Push back off front leg, and lower the dumbbell at shoulder level returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Forward Lunge to Overhead Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding dumbbells at shoulder height with your elbows bent, wrists facing the body.

2.Take a step forward, dropping your back knee towards the floor, at the same time, press the dumbbells overhead, rotating your wrists away from the body, extending arms fully.

3.Push off your front foot and lower the dumbbells to shoulder level returning to starting position.

4.Alternate legs on each repetition.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Forward Lunge with Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, step forward into a lunge while curling the dumbbells to your shoulders at the same time.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Rack Walking Lunge
exercises.description: Holding a dumbbell in each hand at your shoulders (front rack position), step forward into a lunge. Push back to stand and step forward with the other leg, continuing to walk forward with alternating lunges.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Raise
exercises.description:
1) Stand with a dumbbell in each hand, with feet shoulder-width apart. 

2) Lift the weights in front of you, with straight arms, until your forearms are parallel to the floor.

3) Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Squat
exercises.description:
1)  When performing the squat, ensure that your knee, feet and hips are all in line. 

2)  As you sit back, allow your hip to shift back into your heels as your hips drop back and knees stay back behind your toes.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Squat to Arnold Press
exercises.description: Holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing you, lower into a squat. After you stand up, rotate your palms outward and press the dumbbells overhead. Bring them back to the starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front to Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
Stand with a dumbbell in each hand at your thighs. 

Raise both arms straight in front to shoulder height, then open them out to the sides. Lower arms laterally back to start. Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Glute Bridge
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, squeeze your glutes to lift your hips. 

2) Have the dumbbell placed across your hips and hold in place with your arms. 

3) Do your best to keep your abdominals active to not lift and arch from your lower back.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Glute Bridge Alternating Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hold a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, palms facing forward. 

Press through your heels to lift your hips into a glute bridge, forming a straight line from shoulders to knees, keeping your core engaged. From this position, press one dumbbell straight up until your arm is fully extended while keeping the other dumbbell at chest level. 

Lower the dumbbell back to chest level and immediately press the other dumbbell. Continue alternating presses for the desired number of reps. Keep your hips lifted and core tight throughout.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Glute Bridge Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level. Lift your hips into a glute bridge, forming a straight line from your shoulders to your knees. 

While keeping your hips raised, press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended, then lower them back to chest level.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Glute Bridge Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hold a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, palms facing forward. 

Press through your heels to lift your hips into a glute bridge, forming a straight line from shoulders to knees, keeping your core engaged. 

From this position, press the dumbbells straight up until your arms are fully extended, then lower them back to chest level while maintaining your hips lifted.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Glute Bridge Single Arm Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in one hand at chest level. .

Lift your hips into a glute bridge, forming a straight line from your shoulders to your knees. 

While keeping your hips elevated and core tight, press the dumbbell upward until your arm is fully extended, then lower it back to chest level.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Glute Bridge to Sit-Up
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, perform a glute bridge by lifting your hips off the floor, then lower down and smoothly transition into a sit-up, bringing your chest toward your knees.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell vertically close to your chest with both hands, elbows pointing down. Keep your chest up and core engaged. 

Step your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping the dumbbell close to your chest, until your thighs are parallel to the floor (or as low as comfortable). 

Push through your heels to return to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat Pulses
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell vertically at your chest with both hands, elbows pointing down. Step your feet shoulder-width apart. 

Lower your body into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor. From this squat position, pulse up and down slightly by moving only a few inches, keeping tension in your legs and glutes. 

Maintain an upright chest and engaged core throughout.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Half Turkish Get-Up
exercises.description:
Lie on your back holding a dumbbell in one hand, arm fully extended toward the ceiling. Bend the knee on the same side as the dumbbell, keeping the opposite leg straight on the floor. 

Press through your foot and roll onto your opposite elbow, then rise onto your hand while keeping the dumbbell overhead. Push through your foot to lift your hips into a bridge.

Lower back down in reverse order to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
exercises.description:
1) Stand up straight with arms hanging and dumbbells in both hands, palms facing in. 

2) Curl the weight in your right hand up toward your right shoulder, keeping your palm faced inwards at all times. 

3) Pause at the top of the lift and then slowly lower. 

4) Alternate.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl to Neutral Press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, hammer‑curl the weights to your shoulders, then press the weights overhead with a neutral grip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Preacher Curl
exercises.description:
1.Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, holding dumbbells with palms facing each other and arms resting on the pad.

2.Curl the dumbbells up to shoulder height, bending at the elbows. Keep your shoulders steady as your raise the dumbbells.

3.Lower the dumbbells returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hamstring Curl
exercises.description: Dumbbell Hamstring Curl

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hang Power Clean
exercises.description:
1.Hold dumbbells at your knees in a slight squat with your feet hip-width apart and your head up. 

2.ln one motion rapidly extend your hips and knees, pulling the dumbbells up, keeping your arms straight and shrugging your shoulders.

3.Drop down as the dumbbells travel up and absorb them at your shoulders in a low squat.

4.Stand with the dumbbells at your shoulders. Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Heel Elevated Goblet Squat
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell vertically at your chest with both hands, elbows pointing down. Place your heels on a small raised platform (like weight plates or a wedge) while keeping your feet flat on the ground.
 
Step your feet about shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest up and the dumbbell close to your chest, until your thighs are parallel to the floor (or as low as comfortable). 

Push through your heels to return to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Heel Elevated Hack Squat - Katie Mack Fitness
exercises.description: Dumbbell Heel Elevated Hack Squat - Katie Mack Fitness

exercises.name: Dumbbell High Pull
exercises.description:
1.In a slight squat position, hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your arm straight.

2.Extend your hips, lift your legs off the floor with an explosive jump and rapidly raise the dumbbell up to just below your chin, keeping your elbows high.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell High Pull to Press
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Hinge slightly at the hips and bend your knees slightly. Explosively pull the dumbbells upward toward your chin, leading with your elbows (high pull). 

As the dumbbells reach chest height, transition smoothly into an overhead press by pushing the weights straight up until your arms are fully extended. 

Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat for reps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hip Thrust
exercises.alternateName: Dumbbell Bench Hip Thruster Glute Bridge
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, you'll want most of the work being done from your hips rather than your lower back. Keep your abdominal set - particularly at the top of the lift.

2. To stabilized the dumbbell, you'll need to use your hands to make sure it doesn't roll off. 

3. Begin by sitting close to a bench while holding the dumbbells. This will be your starting position.

4. Begin the movement by driving through with your heels, extending your hips vertically keeping the dumbbells in place. Your weight should be supported by your upper back and the heels of your feet.

5. Extend as far as possible.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Alternating Chest Press
exercises.description:
Set an incline bench to 45 degrees and perform an alternating chest press. 

The incline angle targets the upper chest, while the alternating movement adds a stability challenge.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Alternating Curl
exercises.description:
1) Lay back on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing away from the body.

2) Slowly curl the weight in your right hand up to shoulder level, pause, and then slowly lower the weight.

3) Alternate with the other arm.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Alternating Tricep Press
exercises.description:
Perform an incline narrow chest press, alternating arms with each repetition. 

This adds a stability challenge while targeting the upper chest and triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Alternating Row
exercises.description:
Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and lie face down, holding dumbbells. 

Row one dumbbell towards your hip while keeping the other arm extended. 

Lower the dumbbell with control, then alternate to the other arm, focusing on squeezing your back muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Back Fly
exercises.alternateName: Incline Dumbbell Reverse Fly Rear delt
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure that you squeeze your shoulder blades together to lift the dumbbells. 

2) Keep a soft elbow bend. 

3) Lie down on an incline bench. Have the dumbbells in each hand with the palms facing each other. 

4) Move the weights out and away from each other in an arc motion. 

5) The arms should be elevated until they are parallel to the floor.

6) Slowly lower the weights back down. 

7) Repeat.
